title: sphaeradenia garciae subsp. garciae
intro: Sphaeradenia garciae subsp. garciae, a species of flowering plant in the family Velloziaceae, is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is also known as "Yacón" and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to the presence of inulin and other compounds. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, diabetes, and inflammation, and is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Traditionally, the plant's roots are used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to support overall health and well-being.
